## Chapter 24 Soft Drop Tasks

Access the following four tasks to complete the Soft Drop portion of the audit:
Not all tasks are required each time a soft drop is performed.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/281.2.png)




· Actual Soft Drop-Retrieves and displays soft drop information (if available).

· Soft Drop Manual Entry-For manual entry of soft drop details (also called Soft Drop
Edit).

· Correct Soft Drop Errors-To correct soft drop errors.

· Correct Soft Drop Variances-To correct soft drop variances.


### To access the Soft Drop tasks:

Click Soft Drop in the User Tasks list OR Period Timeline. The Soft Drop options list
expands.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="281" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 24 Soft Drop Tasks" -->




![Figure 24-1 User Tasks List-the Soft Drop Tasks](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/282.1.png)




#### 24.1 Actual Soft Drop

Conduct an actual Soft Drop to retrieve soft-drop data from a disk, enter soft-drop data
manually, or confirm no drop for the audit period.


##### To retrieve actual soft drop:

1\. If necessary, select Open Period from the toolbar. The current audit period appears.

2\. Click Soft Drop in the User Tasks list or Period Timeline. A list of options appears.

3\. Click Actual Soft Drop. If soft drop data has been posted, it is retrieved and processed.
OR
If a soft drop does not exist for the accounting period, the Soft Drop Selection window
appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/282.2.png)




4\. Select the appropriate option for retrieving the drop:

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="282" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 24 Soft Drop Tasks" -->

· From Disk-In the case of a network failure, if the actual drop was saved to a disk.

. Enter Drop Manually-Necessary if a failure occurred with the soft drop and
information transfer did not occur.

. No Drop Today-No soft drop was done for the accounting day.

5\. Click OK. A confirmation window appears after the soft drop is retrieved.

6\. Click Done. The retrieved soft drop data appears.

7\. Click OK. The Enter Soft Drop Manual Meters window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/283.1.png)




8\. Select Exceptions. The Enter Soft Drop Manual Meters window displays the soft drops
exceeding the percentage of variance between actual and metered soft drop.

9\. Continue to Adjusting Soft Drops on page 283, if needed.


## 24.2 Adjusting Soft Drops

Soft-drop adjustments include:

· Correcting errors (matching orphan cans to orphan machines)

· Accepting, swapping, and unaccepting variances for specific machines

· Adjusting soft-drop meters for specific machines

· Editing soft-drop amounts for specific machines


### 24.2.1 Correcting Soft-Drop Errors




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/283.2.png)




Access this option only AFTER obtaining the actual soft drop for the period.


#### To correct soft-drop errors:

1\. Click Soft Drop in the User Tasks list or Period Timeline. A list of options appears.

2\. Click Correct Soft Drop Errors. The Correct Soft Drop Errors window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/283.3.png)




3\. Drag and drop the orphan cans onto the appropriate orphan machines.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="283" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 24 Soft Drop Tasks" -->




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/284.1.png)




To un-assign a drop from a machine:

1\. Right-click a drop item.

2\. Click Unassign Drop.

3\. Click Finish Drop Errors after all drops are assigned.


### 24.2.2 Accepting Soft-Drop Variances


#### To accept a soft-drop variance:

1\. Click Soft Drop in the User Tasks list or Period Timeline. A list of options appears.

2\. Click Correct Soft Drop Variance. The Correct Soft Drop Errors window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/284.2.png)




<table>
<tr>
<th colspan="4" rowspan="2">☒ All Dropped Dropped: 1 ☐ Exceptions Actual [$]: 0.00</th>
<th rowspan="2"></th>

3\. Select All Dropped or Exceptions to filter the list of machines.

4\. Select a machine.
OR
Press Ctrl + click to select multiple machines.

5\. Click Accept if the variances are within established guidelines. A confirmation prompt
appears.

6\. Click Yes. The variances are accepted. The drop is removed from the Correct Soft Drop
Variance window.


#### 24.2.3 Swapping Soft-Drop Variances

Swap a variance if, after reviewing soft drop variances, the actual drops for two machines are
posted incorrectly.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="284" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 24 Soft Drop Tasks" -->


#### To swap variances between machines:

1\. Click Soft Drop in the User Tasks list or Period Timeline. A list of options appears.

2\. Click Correct Soft Drop Variance. The Correct Soft Drop Errors window appears.

3\. Select a machine.
OR
Press Ctrl + click to select multiple machines.

4\. Right-click a selected machine, then select Swap Actual Drop to repost the money to
the correct machine. A confirmation prompt appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/285.1.png)




i
An information message displays if the selected machines are not the same
denomination.

5\. Click Yes. The actual drops are swapped.


## 24.2.4 Unaccepting Soft-Drop Variances

To unaccept a variance:

1\. Click Soft Drop in the User Tasks list or Period Timeline. A list of options appears.

2\. Click Correct Soft Drop Variance. The Correct Soft Drop Errors window appears.

3\. Select All Dropped or Exceptions to filter the list of machines.

4\. Select a machine.

5\. Click Unaccept if the variance is not within established guidelines. A confirmation
prompt appears.

6\. Click Yes. The variance is no longer accepted.


## 24.2.5 Adjusting Soft Drops

To adjust a soft drop:

1\. Click Soft Drop in the User Tasks list or Period Timeline. A list of options appears.

2\. Click Correct Soft Drop Variance. The Correct Soft Drop Variance window appears.

3\. Select All Dropped or Exceptions to filter the list of machines.

4\. Select a machine.

5\. Click Actuals. The Soft Drop Edit window appears.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="285" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 24 Soft Drop Tasks" -->




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/286.1.png)




i

Another way to adjust a soft drop is to click Soft Drop on the User Tasks list, then
select Soft Drop Manual Entry. Starting the task this way requires an entry in
the Machine Number field to continue.

6\. Enter the amount actually dropped in the ActualSoft1 field.

7\. Click Save. The adjustments save.


## 24.2.6 Adjusting Soft Meters

A soft-meter reading can be adjusted to balance a soft-drop variance for an individual machine
but is not recommended. The system performs an auto-recalculation when closing the day. This
is when additional adjustments may be necessary. Refer to Adjusting Meter Variances on page
204 for more information.


## 24.3 Assigning Orphan Cans

Auditors can assign orphan cans to orphan machines while correcting soft drops during audits.
For example, while correcting soft drop errors:

1\. An auditor views a list of orphan cans along with a list of orphan machines.

2\. The auditor clicks one of the orphan cans.

3\. The auditor drags the orphan can over a chosen machine and drops it onto that machine.
The orphan can is now applied to that machine.

To access the drag-and-drop feature during the soft-drop audit:

1\. Open a period, then navigate to Soft Drop > Actual Soft Drop.

2\. Select the From Disk method for obtaining the soft drop information.

3\. Click OK. The Correct Soft Drop Errors tab appears.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="286" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 24 Soft Drop Tasks" -->
